name: tropymd
description: Export Tropy items as Markdown files (one per item) with YAML frontmatter, suitable for Obsidian and other Markdown editors.

options.label: Output directory
options.hint: Absolute path to the folder where one Markdown file per item will be written. Leave blank to be prompted at export time. Existing files are not overwritten — re-running the export skips items that already have a file with a matching content hash.

options.label: Workflow tags to drop (comma-separated)
options.hint: Tags in this list are filtered out of the export entirely. Useful for tags you use only as workflow markers (e.g., "to-obsidian" to flag items ready for export).

options.label: Include photo paths in frontmatter
options.hint: Adds a `photos:` field listing each photo's file path. Useful for embedding images in your Markdown notes or linking back from your vault.

options.label: Skip items with no notes
options.hint: When enabled, items that have no note content are not exported (frontmatter-only files are skipped). Useful when you only care about items where you've added analytical content.

options.label: Tag prefix dispatch
options.hint: Comma-separated list of `prefix/=field` rules. Tags matching a listed prefix are extracted (with the prefix stripped) into the named YAML field; unlisted tags stay in the flat `tags:` list. Use `prefix/` alone to use the prefix as the field name. Two prefixes can route to the same field. Example: `person/=people, place/=locations, org/=organization, gov/=organization, theme/=themes`. Leave blank to disable dispatch entirely.

options.label: Wrap dispatched values as [[wiki-links]]
options.hint: When enabled, values in dispatched entity fields are wrapped as [[name]] (Obsidian/Roam/Logseq style). When disabled, they're emitted as plain strings. Has no effect unless tag prefix dispatch is configured.
